term: so ekaṁ vedanaṁ vedayati—kāyikaṁ, na cetasikaṁ
preferred_translation: one feels one feeling: bodily, not mental
definition: SN 36.6's trained painful-feeling line for bodily feeling without added mental reaction.
notes: Use this record for the one-feeling contrast in SN 36.6. Keep the negated mental side explicit so the one-arrow simile remains a practical contrast with reactive doubling.

example_phrases.pali: so ekaṁ vedanaṁ vedayati—kāyikaṁ, na cetasikaṁ
example_phrases.translation: one feels one feeling: bodily, not mental

authority_basis.scope: Supports the one-feeling line that names bodily feeling without added mental reaction.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the trained one-feeling contrast in SN 36.6.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the line into generic calm language or erase the bodily / mental distinction.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the one-arrow teaching from becoming vague composure language instead of a precise bodily / mental feeling contrast.
